Added a new Circuit Breaker Panel Creator System which includes toolbars linked to library objects. The system takes a note which automatically detects the room it is in. if you attached the note to an appliance or a cabinet with an appliance in it then it should automatically parse the name of the appliance into the panel creator. This generates an electrical panel schedule which is included in the ProArch + NCS Layouts. The existing garbage disposal/dishwasher/hood vent outlets also report to the panel schedule

Walls were rebuilt to get their main layer line parameters from a new layer called Walls, Main Layer Lines. This gives the ability to quickly either show the framing lines in wall types or keep them hidden and make it view dependent

BWP tool changed draw order for better display in framing views
MAJOR overhaul of all macro driven systems have led to a decrease in drawing lag of approximately 350ms per click for drawing rooms and placing windows/doors/cabinets are as follows:

Window/Door/Cabinet label systems have all been turned into global methods and have been re-written to call other global methods. This reduces the time it takes for each window/door/cabinet to pass through their logical arguments.
Global helper macros were created to remove bottlenecks
The roof and foundation NFA and the Area Analysis systems were all combined into single macros to reduce redundant calls
Anno tool has been completely re-written for more easily customizable annotations and now automatically reports floor surface when not connected but inside of a room.
Most macro systems are now global lambdas with a reporting output so you can see the output but the call is done through a global which allows objects to retrieve values concurrently
Removed lesser used or obscure macros for easier management in the TMM console
Changed order of calls and dependencies to avoid macro checks that were unnecessary

Rewrote the room and ceiling height macros again and now they automatically format themselves based on the room sizes. The truncate tool now will revert that formatting in the case that you want a full size label to place outside of a small room
All objects now work by looking at a global label system to start parsing their individual values instead of one-by-one iterating through a macro in the TMM thus waiting its turn for the next
Overhauled the Detail Designations Page. Added new categories for deck structural elements and others
Removed the cadlabel fill-based designation system, rewrote details to remove this system.
Built a library of annotations for integrating into the system
Redesigned the detail designations page to simplify it. The detail preview has now been removed
New Construction dimension defaults have no been set to centerline of door openings and single side of main wall layer
Windows now have callouts in elevation and kitchen SPVs instead of labels. Labels exist in every other view type
New callout tool drives details from being connected to a wall

Overhaul of the roof settings for a new modern look and feel. Replaced the roof texture with a tintable comp shingle roof, Improved the bump map. Added new fried boards and gable end shadow board moldings. Removed z bar from gable end and changed the overhang. Changed default materials. Added a gutter guard
Added new casings to windows and doors and a new custom sill profile for a clean look
Added a new exterior siding texture that looks more realistic
Added a new concrete texture with ao maps for pronounced shadows
Aggregated plan materials and reduced some file sizes
Added new framing back clip for easier cross sections
Second overhaul of all layer based fill styles and layer sets including overhaul of all built in wall types
Replaced png files with webp files in the library for better file sizes
Updated helper macros for schedules that use alphanumeric labels

Built-in wall types are now split by display layer and line styles as well as fill styles are derived from the display layers. Wall display of built in walls are now infinitely customizable from layer set to layer set

The entire Pro Plan uses RabsCustom for remapped fonts. If you have a font request I can build it for you. Put it in suggestions. Please re-install the new RabsCustom font and overwrite existing. Please do this prior to installing the Pro Plan

Re-built the remap_small_font macro so it can be applied in regular text, schedules, and more. Check the Project preferences for more info. This means rich text like looking outputs in schedules and anywhere where we canâ€™t or donâ€™t want to use rich text

Window/Door and Cabinet Schedules have been completely rebuilt with the font remapping and look much improved. Enjoy the only Rich-text like schedules on the market. Their columns to include have been renamed for clarity on input location.Their is a custom macro that generates automatic spacing in both the cabinet and window/door labels so that all rows remain spaced equal and keep alignment on schedule wrapping. This makes for very clean schedules that look organized and better than ever

Ai messed up the CSV reading system. This version patches it and updates it. It is far too difficult to allow the â€œ â€“ â€œ method for subfolders and maintain the source code since ai gets confused and messes up parts of the code. The new convention only uses underscores and periods to designate subfolders. Space-dash-space can now be a part of your folder naming convention if you like
Updated the schedule for fixture installs to show column names for reference on its proper use.

Library: The Area Map has now been contained within a Pro Plan CAD detail for easier updating of all Layouts

Kitchen Tools Macro Conflict Mitigation: Major efforts undertaken to resolve macro conflictsâ€”particularly in the kitchen tools package, covering symbols and style palette tools. While some macro conflicts remain in other packages, kitchen tools were the focus of this update.

Annotation Tool Default Values: Introduced new default values for the Annot Tool field in most kitchen tools. For example, the base double bass drawer cabinetâ€™s annotation now automatically reads â€œpots and pansâ€ for new plan and â€œdemoâ€ for as-built plans when linked to the Annot Tool or default note tool. This logic extends to numerous kitchen style palette tools, streamlining kitchen design annotation and reducing redundant tasks for designers.

Note Tool Enhancement: The note tool was rewritten to recognize annotation types within the Annot Tool field (custom fields in the Object Information panel). Proposed and as-built annotations are now separated by a semicolon (;). For example: pots and pans; demo will show â€œpots and pansâ€ in a proposed plan and â€œdemoâ€ in an as-built plan.
Base Cabinet Label Improvements: The base cabinet label now intelligently detects cook topsâ€”if a cabinet has an appliance inserted in its top, it is no longer automatically labeled â€œsink base.â€
Kitchen Tool Enhancements: Various kitchen tools received additional functions and bug fixes, increasing their reliability and feature set.
Fixed Climate Zone macro to report immediately from the CSV file
Fixed misaligned detail page on Arrow Layout
The wall cabinet and full height cabinet labels got destroyed by an AI edit and I just now caught it. Returned function for recognizing wall cabinets properly now and added some function as well
Plan Template: Updated
CSV system supports proper subolders such as Project name John, Subfolder Kitchen. See screenshot below
Pro Plan has been reorganized for faster visual recognition of folder structure
CSV system now has a switch to turn it off and revert to Designer/Client Information based input. You can reformat any field by using a colon to create a name value pair such as
CSV system now supports periods INSTEAD of â€œ â€“ â€œ if the period is present
Here are examples:
Project Name: ARS â€“ Alex.Project Folders.Test will look in /ARS â€“ Alex/Project Folders/Test
Project Name: ARS-345 â€“ Test will look in /ARS/Test
Project Name: Alex â€“ Client-24.Test will look in /Alex â€“ Client/Test

So if a period is present, logic for space minus space is circumvented. If no period is present, space minus space creates a subfolder. In both cases a minus will omit anything after the minus up until another subfolder rule
Keynotes have all new logic. For example, the Floor Plan Keynotes will auto change their name to Demo Keynotes if in the AB file. You can change the name in columns to include of the schedule

Annotation Tool Default Values: Introduced new default values for the Annot Tool field in most kitchen tools. For example, the base double bass drawer cabinetâ€™s annotation now automatically reads â€œpots and pansâ€ for new plan and â€œdemoâ€ for as-built plans when linked to the Annot Tool or default note tool. This logic extends to numerous kitchen style palette tools, streamlining kitchen design annotation and reducing redundant tasks for designers.

Project CSV File System Update: The project CSV file system now accepts periods (.) in addition to â€œspace-dash-spaceâ€ for distinguishing subfolders. It also accepts partial matches if it finds no matches as well as a better implementation of searching folder structures. This has not been tested on Mac but should work far better than previous versions.

This change supports broader folder naming conventions, including the use of â€œspace-dash-spaceâ€ in custom folder names outside Chief Architect. Fuzzy matching was improved to better handle extra spaces in project file names.
Such as John.ADA as a project name will look in the /John/ADA/ folder
Fixed header label for No Work condition to report N/A to schedule

The Keynote Schedules now have a new trick implemented. They are using a conditional title for the keynote that changes wether the plan is an existing plan or new plan. For example, the floor plan keynotes will display demo keynotes on your -AB plan
Layout Files: Guide points have been added to logo replacement and an engineer stamp input has been added to the logo field. Please delete the stamp if you do not use the stamp, this is the easiest way to remove it from all layouts
The Area Map has now been contained within a Pro Plan CAD detail for easier updating of all Layouts

The CSV system ignores any file name after the â€œLayoutâ€ folder it exists in, but if your project name is ARS â€“ Alex. And you make a subfolder of your project such as â€œKitchenâ€ then the CSV will look in /ARS â€“ Alex/Kitchen/
Fixed misaligned detail page on Arrow Layout

Library: Door Label Macro Error Fix
- Fixed critical "undefined method 'empty?' for nil:NilClass" error in door labels
- Added comprehensive nil safety checks throughout door label macro
- Fixed issue where door types configuration was not being properly referenced from all options
- Restored proper door type handling that was missing from previous AI-generated code
- Added fallback values and safe property access for doors in non-standard spaces
- Note: This corrects an error introduced by previous AI-based coding that hardcoded door logic instead of using the configurable all options system
- Added enhanced label_Rab macro with comprehensive error handling and superscript fallbacks
- Added label_Super helper macro for dimension formatting with superscript support
- Implemented rescue wrappers for doors, windows, and cabinets with evaluation errors
- Cabinet fallback format: schedule_number + type[0] + width + height
- Door/Window fallback format: schedule_number + superscript dimensions in typical label fashion
- Restored proper functionality of user options for door labels
- Applied same space character handling to cabinet label replacement fields
Plumbing Keynotes CAD Detail Restoration
- Added back plumbing keynotes CAD detail to the system
- Restored plumbing annotation capabilities for detailed plumbing documentation

- Climate zone macros are automatically resolved before applying small font remapping
- Repaired Willow layouts by removing fill style from sheet size text

- Added comprehensive interior door schedule functionality
- Integrated door schedule with layout system for floor plan views
- Applied door schedule across all Architectural and ANSI D layouts

- Error was occurring specifically in attic/unfinished spaces on 2nd floor
Enhanced Label System with Error Handling
- Added label_Super helper macro for dimension formatting with superscript support
- Cabinet fallback format: schedule_number + type[0] + width + height
- Door/Window fallback format: schedule_number + superscript dimensions in typical label fashion
- All fallbacks maintain proper superscript formatting for dimensions
Label Replacement and User Options Enhancements
- Fixed label replacement field to accept single space character as valid input
- Single space character now properly clears both type and hinge side labels
- Hinge side display now correctly respects user option settings
- Example: Entering " " (single space) in Label Replacement will show only dimensions without type or handiness
- Example label output: "2668" instead of "2668 RH" when space is used
- Maintains existing plumbing keynote functionality and formatting
Wall Legend Restoration (Performance Optimized)
- Added back wall legend functionality to the system
- **Important Note:** Wall legend is currently set to NOT include walls by default
- This prevents live updating and wall legend population during normal plan work
- **Action Required Before Printing:** Open the wall legend and check "Include Walls" option to populate the legend
- Performance optimization prevents potential slowdowns during plan development
- Wall legend will only update when manually enabled for final documentation
